jokes.text: You'll never have to experience me again.
jokes.setup: Larry is terrible at golf and his friends are trying to be encouraging
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating response is unnecessarily dramatic and bitter
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - turning a minor social interaction into existential drama. The unexpected darkness makes it funny.

jokes.text: It was a little boring, to be honest with you... well, it fucking was.
jokes.setup: Friend asks if Larry had fun playing golf with them
jokes.punchline: Larry brutally honest response despite social expectation to be polite
jokes.explanation: Larry's inability to follow basic social norms - he doubles down on the insult when called out.

jokes.text: You paid $150 for me to look like a fucking asshole?
jokes.setup: Friend mentions paying Larry's guest fee for golf
jokes.punchline: Larry reframes it as paying to watch him humiliate himself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - takes the payment issue and makes it about dignity and embarrassment.

jokes.text: You're gonna use your child to get out of a party?... It's the best thing in the world... It's a great reason to have kids.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests lying about his sick child to avoid a party
jokes.punchline: Larry turns parenting into a calculated social manipulation tool
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest observation about parenting perks. Larry's enthusiasm for the manipulation is what makes it funny.

jokes.text: I'll just show up tonight and pretend I had the wrong night... Ding-dong-- 'where's the party?'
jokes.setup: Larry needs excuse for missing one party to go to another
jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan to fake confusion about party dates
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central premise. The 'ding-dong' detail shows Larry's theatrical planning.

jokes.text: Have you ever seen Richard look in the mirror?... Is that really what I do?
jokes.setup: Jeff asks if Larry has seen Richard look in mirror
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately starts checking himself in mirror, proving Jeff's point
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy - Larry's lack of self-awareness demonstrated through immediate mirror behavior.

jokes.text: Cha cha... where did he get her from? Man, oh, man. She is so hot.
jokes.setup: Discussion about going to Danson's party
jokes.punchline: Larry's fixation on Richard's hot girlfriend Cha Cha
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's attraction to Cha Cha which pays off later. The repeated 'Cha cha' emphasizes his fixation.

jokes.text: I'm gonna dress like a little Dutch girl.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks what Larry will wear to the party
jokes.punchline: Completely random, absurd response delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - completely unexpected response. Classic Larry non-sequitur humor.

jokes.text: 'Cha cha.' 'Cha cha.' Discussion of Richard's hot girlfriend
jokes.setup: Mention of Richard's girlfriend's unusual name
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of the name 'Cha cha' and their appreciation of her
jokes.explanation: Setup for later payoff. The name itself is mildly amusing but this plants it.

jokes.text: Schmohawks. Did I ever tell you that?... 'Hey, schmohawk!'
jokes.setup: Larry explains what his father called certain people
jokes.punchline: The made-up Yiddish insult 'schmohawk' and Larry's fond delivery
jokes.explanation: Great invented word that sounds authentically Yiddish. Larry's nostalgia for his father's random insults is very character-specific.

jokes.text: I would need a couple of months to mentally prepare for that. Tomorrow?
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggesting they house hurricane victims tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic overreaction to the timeline
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Larry's need for control and preparation time for basic human kindness.

jokes.text: What do I have? I don't know what's so-- what do I have? I like this sport jacket. I can't replace this.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks Larry to imagine losing everything he has
jokes.punchline: Larry can only think of his sport jacket as irreplaceable possession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-absorption - can't empathize with hurricane victims, only thinks about his jacket.

jokes.text: I would need a couple of months to mentally prepare for that. Tomorrow?
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggests housing hurricane family starting tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Larry's need for extensive mental preparation time
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic need to over-prepare for social situations, even charitable ones.

jokes.text: Oh, they've been gone for about 24 hours. The party was last night.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl arrive expecting to find party in progress
jokes.punchline: Funkhouser reveals party was 24 hours ago
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of Larry's plan backfiring. The '24 hours' detail makes it more absurd.

jokes.text: This may be the dumbest thing I've ever done in my life-- well, we've ever done.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes his wrong-night plan is transparent
jokes.punchline: Larry admits stupidity then tries to spread blame to Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Larry's quick pivot from self-blame to shared blame is very characteristic. Good cringe moment.

jokes.text: My mother would be horrified-- horrified if she knew that I was sitting in the living room the night after a big party just took place-- horrified.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to leave Funkhouser's house
jokes.punchline: Larry's excessive concern about his dead mother's judgment on party etiquette
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic projection of his own anxiety onto his deceased mother. The triple 'horrified' is good escalation.

jokes.text: I left the car running... If you walk away from the car, it's on, and you got your car keys in your hand, it goes 'beep beep beep beep beep.'
jokes.setup: Larry claims he left car running as excuse to leave
jokes.punchline: Ted explains why that's impossible with modern car technology
jokes.explanation: Good technical comedy - Larry's lie exposed by car features. Ted's detailed 'beep beep beep' sound effect is funny.

jokes.text: 1000 pick-up sticks... I go get the sticks, I throw them all over the room and we pick up the sticks.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser suggests a game to play
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up absurd game that's just picking up scattered sticks
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Funkhouser invention. The mundane nature of the 'game' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What are the odds?... Yeah, what are the odds?
jokes.setup: Jeff shows up with same wrong-night excuse as Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry and Jeff pretend coincidence while knowing it's planned
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's scheme. Their fake amazement at the 'coincidence' is nicely played.

jokes.text: Jeff and Susie arriving with the same 'wrong night' excuse
jokes.setup: Larry's original deception
jokes.punchline: Jeff steals the exact same lie
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Larry's lie becomes epidemic, trapping him further.

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? You stole my wrong-night bit.
jokes.setup: Larry confronts Jeff about using the same excuse
jokes.punchline: Larry treats the lie like intellectual property theft
jokes.explanation: Larry's proprietary attitude about his social deceptions. Calling it a 'bit' shows his theatrical mindset.

jokes.text: I'm sucking on it like a mint.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing how good the cake is
jokes.punchline: Jeff's bizarre method of eating cake
jokes.explanation: Weird eating behavior that somehow makes sense for Jeff's character. Visual comedy in the absurd technique.

jokes.text: My daughter is waiting for a puppet show... My dad has a virus. He's in bed with fever, with covers up to his neck.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to create excuses to leave
jokes.punchline: Larry piles on increasingly specific and dramatic fake emergencies
jokes.explanation: Larry's escalating desperation shown through increasingly elaborate lies. The specific details make it funnier.

jokes.text: Your dad always has a virus, okay? Fuck you, he doesn't always have a virus.
jokes.setup: Jeff calls out Larry's pattern of lying about his father
jokes.punchline: Larry's angry defense of his fictional sick father
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Larry gets genuinely offended about his fake excuse being called out as a pattern.

jokes.text: We're going into the living room and we're gonna play 'The Newlywed Game.'
jokes.setup: Funkhouser announces the next activity
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate party game that will create marital tension
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for disaster. Funkhouser's enthusiasm for potentially marriage-destroying games is very character-specific.

jokes.text: If you could have sex with one of your friends' wives or girlfriends, who would it be?
jokes.setup: Funkhouser reads Newlywed Game question
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate question guaranteed to cause problems
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe setup - question designed to create maximum discomfort and relationship damage.

jokes.text: I've gotta go with my little poofer.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser answering who he'd have sex with
jokes.punchline: Funkhouser's creepy pet name for his wife
jokes.explanation: Disturbing pet name reveals Funkhouser's weird relationship dynamic. 'Poofer' is unsettling.

jokes.text: I had no idea you've been-- that you wanted to have sex with cha cha... Obviously, there's a fantasy going down.
jokes.setup: Cheryl processing Larry's answer
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's dawning realization of Larry's attraction
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of marital tension. Cheryl's hurt and suspicion feels authentic and awkward.

jokes.text: You love your wives. Fuck you and fuck you.
jokes.setup: Men claiming they love their wives after inappropriate game
jokes.punchline: Susie's angry dismissal of their damage control
jokes.explanation: Susie cuts through the BS with characteristic directness. Her double 'fuck you' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Short of adopting this hurricane family.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he'll do anything to make up with Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Larry's exception reveals his real priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that seals Larry's fate. His 'anything except the one thing she actually wants' is peak Larry selfishness.

jokes.text: They thought the party was tonight... You're kidding... All right, it's good to see you.
jokes.setup: Third instance of wrong-night excuse at Ted's house
jokes.punchline: Pattern now clearly established as running gag
jokes.explanation: Third repetition makes it a clear running gag. Diminishing returns but still functional.

jokes.text: I was raised not to intrude on people. My mother would be horrified-- horrified
jokes.setup: Larry again trying to leave Ted's house
jokes.punchline: Second reference to his mother being horrified
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier mother reference, but less effective the second time. Still character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Larry's elaborate explanation about being raised not to intrude and his mother's horror
jokes.setup: Larry defending his discomfort at staying after missing the party
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic explanation of basic social etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good Larry neurosis - overanalyzing social situation through his mother's imagined reaction.

jokes.text: Richard Lewis arriving and immediately using the same wrong-night excuse
jokes.setup: The wrong-night excuse has now been used three times
jokes.punchline: A fourth person independently has the 'same' mistake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the lie has become epidemic, making it seem less suspicious but more absurd.

jokes.text: What did you steal my thing for?... Showing-up-on-the-wrong-night thing... You're making a fool out of me.
jokes.setup: Richard confronts Larry about copying his excuse
jokes.punchline: Both are angry about intellectual property theft of the same lie
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - now multiple people are angry about the stolen excuse scheme.

jokes.text: Come on, schmohawk!
jokes.setup: Larry driving away from Ted's house
jokes.punchline: Larry uses his father's insult on other drivers
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier schmohawk story. Shows Larry adopting his father's behavior.

jokes.text: Meeting the Black family at the airport with all their luggage
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl picking up hurricane victims
jokes.punchline: The visual of meeting the family and awkward introductions
jokes.explanation: Setup for future episodes - meeting the family is more setup than punchline.

jokes.text: It's like if my last name was Jew, like Larry Jew-- 'cause I'm Jewish... And I'm Jewish.
jokes.setup: Larry meeting the Black family at airport
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredibly awkward attempt to relate to their name
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry awkwardness. His complete inability to handle racial dynamics with any grace is perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: Auntie Rae smoking in the house despite the no-smoking rule
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl establish house rules
jokes.punchline: Immediate violation with Larry's awkward reaction
jokes.explanation: Functional setup for ongoing conflict - immediate rule violation establishes dynamics.

jokes.text: We don't smoke in the house... cigarette... smoking...
jokes.setup: Larry notices Auntie Rae smoking inside
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete inability to directly address the smoking issue
jokes.explanation: Larry's conflict avoidance in action. His fragmented speech shows his discomfort with confrontation.

jokes.text: That's a penis... Oh my God... Funkhouser-- he knowingly served us penis.
jokes.setup: Larry sees the cake at the erotic bakery
jokes.punchline: Realization that Funkhouser deliberately served them penis cake
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback revelation that recontextualizes earlier cake scene. Larry's moral outrage is hilarious.

jokes.text: I told you it was a black log cake.
jokes.setup: Larry accusing Marty of deception about the penis cake
jokes.punchline: Marty's technically truthful description
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Marty was technically honest. The euphemistic description becomes funnier in hindsight.

jokes.text: Why did you take the balls home?... I didn't know they were balls.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser confronts Larry about taking cake leftovers
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent explanation for taking testicle cake
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up to penis cake reveal. Larry's innocence about taking 'balls' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: My friend Jeff did not know-- did not know that there was a penis in that box when he picked that up from the bakery.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining penis cake incident to Black family
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate attempt to explain the unexplainable
jokes.explanation: Larry's desperate damage control. Trying to explain penis cake to guests is peak awkwardness.

jokes.text: To see a big penis like that-- what are you gonna do? She's a little girl.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing traumatized child
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact assessment of penis trauma
jokes.explanation: Larry's casual discussion of child penis trauma is wonderfully inappropriate. His practical tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Ate some penis.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks where Larry has been
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact summary of the evening
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and economy. Larry's casual summary of the absurd evening is quotable gold.

jokes.text: Maybe some family will adopt us.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl discover their house burned down
jokes.punchline: Complete role reversal - now they need housing
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback. Larry's reluctance to help hurricane victims comes full circle when he needs help himself.

jokes.text: The party was yesterday... Oh, you're kidding me.
jokes.setup: Final wrong-night appearance at burned house
jokes.punchline: Wrong-night gag continues even at disaster scene
jokes.explanation: Final iteration of wrong-night gag. Even Larry's house burning down doesn't stop the social awkwardness.
